sourceMovie.title: Child's Play
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Child's Play (1988) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Tom Holland's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Scream
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Child's Play clicked for you, Scream (1996) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Child's Play clicked for you, Halloween (1978) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Child's Play?
faq.answer: Try Scream, Halloween, Annabelle: Creation—each shares DNA with Child's Play while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Child's Play?
faq.answer: Child's Play (1988) is best described as Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Child's Play part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Child's Play Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
